title: Session Manager
intro: All Codex Desktop sessions across every provider. Codex tags each session with one provider and filters its list by it — so switching providers in mimo2codex hides the others. Here you see them all, and can migrate a session to another provider.
localOnly: Session management reads your local ~/.codex and is only available in local (desktop / CLI) mode, not in a server deployment.
unavailable: Couldn't read Codex's session database (~/.codex/state_*.sqlite). It appears once you've run Codex Desktop at least once.
danger: Migration edits Codex's own session database. Fully quit Codex Desktop first, and mimo2codex will back up the database before changing anything.
migrateBody: Codex will list this session under the target provider after you restart it. mimo2codex backs up the session database first. Make sure Codex Desktop is fully closed.
batchMigrateTitle: Migrate selected sessions to another provider
batchResult: Migrated {{ok}}, failed {{fail}}. Restart Codex Desktop to see them under the new provider.
migrated: Session migrated: {{from}} → {{to}}. Restart Codex Desktop to see it under the new provider.
codexRunning: Codex Desktop appears to be running. Fully quit it, then try again — editing its database while open could corrupt your sessions.
transcriptEmpty: This session has no readable messages.
transcriptUnavailable: Couldn't read this session's rollout file.
